questions.text: The option that shows three base quantities’ units in System International (SI) is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because 'gram' and 'joule' are derived units. The base SI unit for mass is 'kilogram', and 'joule' is a derived unit of energy.
This option is incorrect as all units listed are from the CGS system. 'Gram' and 'centimetre' are not SI base units, and 'dyne' is a derived unit of force in the CGS system.
This is the correct option. 'Kilogram', 'metre', and 'second' are the base SI units for mass, length, and time, respectively.
This option is incorrect because, although 'kilogram' and 'second' are base SI units, 'newton' is a derived unit of force.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C: 'Kilogram, metre, and second'. These are the base units in the SI system for the fundamental quantities of mass, length, and time. Option D is incorrect because 'newton' is a derived unit of force. Options A and B list units that are either derived or belong to the CGS system, which are not considered SI base units.

questions.text: The second equation of motion of a uniformly accelerated ball along a straight line is stated ass = vft +1/2 at2The dimension of this equation is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This represents the dimension of length, which is incorrect as we need to consider all terms in the equation.
This represents the dimension of time, which is incorrect as the equation involves multiple physical quantities with different dimensions.
This represents the dimension of velocity, which is incorrect as the equation involves more than just velocity.
This option indicates the dimensions of the entire equation when all terms are considered, which is correct.

questions.explanation: The second equation of motion is s = vft + 1/2 at2. The dimension of the first term vft is [L][T-1][T] = [L]. The dimension of the second term 1/2 at2 is [L][T-2][T2] = [L]. Since both terms have the same dimensions, the overall dimension of the equation is [L]. Option D is therefore the correct answer. Options A, B, and C are incorrect as they do not represent the dimensional analysis of the entire equation.

questions.text: If the tail of the first vector coincides with the head of the second vector, the resultant of twovectors will be found by
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Vector division is not a standard operation in vector algebra.
When the tail of one vector coincides with the head of another, the vectors are added.
Vector subtraction involves reversing the direction of one vector before adding, not applicable here.
Vector multiplication (dot or cross product) does not apply to this method of combining vectors.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is addition of vectors. When the tail of the first vector coincides with the head of the second vector, they are positioned for vector addition. This method is known as the 'head-to-tail' rule, where the resultant vector is drawn from the tail of the first vector to the head of the second vector. Division, subtraction, and multiplication of vectors do not fit this scenario. Vector division is not defined, subtraction requires reversing a vector, and multiplication involves different operations like dot or cross products.

questions.text: If a vector is multiplied by a negative number, then its direction will
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When a vector is multiplied by a negative number, it reverses direction.
Multiplying by a negative number doesn't cause a fixed angular change like 45o.
A 90o change in direction is not the result of multiplying a vector by a negative number.
The direction changes when a vector is multiplied by a negative number; it does not remain the same.

questions.explanation: When a vector is multiplied by a negative number, its direction is reversed. This is equivalent to rotating the vector by 180o, but more simply described as a reversal of direction. Hence, Option A is correct. Options B and C suggest specific angular changes that do not occur as a result of such multiplication, while Option D incorrectly states that the direction remains unchanged.

questions.text: If both the components of a vector are negative, then the vector lies in the
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The first quadrant contains vectors with both components positive.
The second quadrant contains vectors with a negative x-component and a positive y-component.
The third quadrant contains vectors with both components negative.
The fourth quadrant contains vectors with a positive x-component and a negative y-component.

questions.explanation: In the Cartesian coordinate system, the third quadrant is characterized by both x and y components being negative. Thus, a vector with both negative components lies in the third quadrant. Other quadrants have different combinations of positive and negative components: the first quadrant has both positive, the second quadrant has a negative x-component and a positive y-component, and the fourth quadrant has a positive x-component and a negative y-component.

questions.text: The vector product of moment arm ř and force F^ is called torque.The direction of torque is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect because the torque is not along the force vector. Torque is perpendicular to the force vector and is determined by the cross product of the moment arm and the force.
This is incorrect because torque is also not along the moment arm vector. It is perpendicular to both the moment arm and the force vectors.

options.text: Parallel to the plane containing r and F
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Torque is never parallel to the plane formed by the moment arm and force vectors. Instead, it is perpendicular to it.

options.text: Perpendicular to the plane containing r and F
options.explanation: This is correct. Torque is the result of the cross product of the moment arm and the force, and its direction is always perpendicular to the plane formed by these two vectors.

questions.explanation: * Torque (τ) is the vector product (or cross product) of the moment arm (r) and the force (F), represented as τ = r × F.* The direction of a vector product is always perpendicular to the plane formed by the two vectors being multiplied. In this case, the torque vector is perpendicular to the plane containing both the moment arm vector (r) and the force vector (F).

questions.text: A paratrooper falling with uniform velocity is an example of
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Static equilibrium occurs when an object is at rest and the net forces acting on it are zero. Since the paratrooper is moving, this option is incorrect.

Stable equilibrium refers to an object's tendency to return to its equilibrium position after a small disturbance, typically when it is at rest. The paratrooper is in motion, so this option is unsuitable.

Unstable equilibrium is when an object moves further away from its equilibrium position after a disturbance. This is generally not applicable to objects in motion like the paratrooper at constant velocity.

Dynamic equilibrium occurs when an object moves at a constant velocity, meaning the net force acting on it is zero. For the paratrooper, terminal velocity is reached when air resistance equals gravitational force, resulting in zero acceleration.

questions.explanation: Dynamic equilibrium occurs when an object is moving at a constant velocity (zero acceleration) and the net force acting on it is zero. In the case of a paratrooper, once they reach terminal velocity, the force of air resistance balances the force of gravity, resulting in a net force of zero and constant downward velocity.

questions.text: An object falls freely from rest. The total distance covered by it in 2 s will be(Note: The acceleration due to gravity ‘g’ is 9.8 m/s2.)
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option results from incorrectly using the formula with only half the correct time, i.e., using 1 second instead of 2 seconds.
This is the correct answer. Using the kinematic equation s = ut + (1/2)at², with u = 0 m/s, t = 2 s, and a = 9.8 m/s², we get s = (1/2)(9.8)(4) = 19.6 m.
This option results from a miscalculation or misunderstanding of the formula, possibly by doubling the correct distance without justification.
This option results from misapplying the formula as if the object had an initial velocity or from doubling the correct distance erroneously.

questions.explanation: To determine the total distance covered by an object falling freely from rest, we use the following kinematic equation:* s = ut + (1/2)at²Where:* s = distance covered* u = initial velocity* t = time* a = acceleration (in this case, acceleration due to gravity, g)Given values from the question:* Initial velocity (u) = 0 m/s (since it falls from rest)* Time (t) = 2 s* Acceleration due to gravity (g) = 9.8 m/s²Now, substitute these values into the formula:* s = (0 m/s)(2 s) + (1/2)(9.8 m/s²)(2 s)²* s = 0 + (1/2)(9.8)(4)* s = (1/2)(39.2)* s = 19.6 m

questions.text: Which of the following shows the dimensions of force and acceleration respectively?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Force (F) is defined by Newton's second law as mass (m) multiplied by acceleration (a) (F = ma).* The dimension of mass is [M] (Mass).* The dimension of acceleration is [L][T]^-2 (Length per time squared).Therefore, the dimension of Force is [M][L][T]^-2.Dimensions of Acceleration:Acceleration (a) is the rate of change of velocity, which is velocity per unit time. Velocity is defined as displacement per unit time.* The dimension of displacement (length) is [L].* The dimension of time is [T].* So, the dimension of velocity is [L][T]^-1.* Therefore, the dimension of acceleration is [L][T]^-1 / [T] = [L][T]^-2.

Force (F) is defined by Newton's second law as mass (m) multiplied by acceleration (a) (F = ma).* The dimension of mass is [M] (Mass).* The dimension of acceleration is [L][T]^-2 (Length per time squared).Therefore, the dimension of Force is [M][L][T]^-2.Dimensions of Acceleration:Acceleration (a) is the rate of change of velocity, which is velocity per unit time. Velocity is defined as displacement per unit time.* The dimension of displacement (length) is [L].* The dimension of time is [T].* So, the dimension of velocity is [L][T]^-1.* Therefore, the dimension of acceleration is [L][T]^-1 / [T] = [L][T]^-2.

Force (F) is defined by Newton's second law as mass (m) multiplied by acceleration (a) (F = ma).* The dimension of mass is [M] (Mass).* The dimension of acceleration is [L][T]^-2 (Length per time squared).Therefore, the dimension of Force is [M][L][T]^-2.Dimensions of Acceleration:Acceleration (a) is the rate of change of velocity, which is velocity per unit time. Velocity is defined as displacement per unit time.* The dimension of displacement (length) is [L].* The dimension of time is [T].* So, the dimension of velocity is [L][T]^-1.* Therefore, the dimension of acceleration is [L][T]^-1 / [T] = [L][T]^-2.

Force (F) is defined by Newton's second law as mass (m) multiplied by acceleration (a) (F = ma).* The dimension of mass is [M] (Mass).* The dimension of acceleration is [L][T]^-2 (Length per time squared).Therefore, the dimension of Force is [M][L][T]^-2.Dimensions of Acceleration:Acceleration (a) is the rate of change of velocity, which is velocity per unit time. Velocity is defined as displacement per unit time.* The dimension of displacement (length) is [L].* The dimension of time is [T].* So, the dimension of velocity is [L][T]^-1.* Therefore, the dimension of acceleration is [L][T]^-1 / [T] = [L][T]^-2.

questions.explanation: Force (F) is defined by Newton's second law as mass (m) multiplied by acceleration (a) (F = ma).* The dimension of mass is [M] (Mass).* The dimension of acceleration is [L][T]^-2 (Length per time squared).Therefore, the dimension of Force is [M][L][T]^-2.Dimensions of Acceleration:Acceleration (a) is the rate of change of velocity, which is velocity per unit time. Velocity is defined as displacement per unit time.* The dimension of displacement (length) is [L].* The dimension of time is [T].* So, the dimension of velocity is [L][T]^-1.* Therefore, the dimension of acceleration is [L][T]^-1 / [T] = [L][T]^-2.

questions.text: The length of a strip is measured by a metre scale as 3.4 cm. The reading measured on the Vernier callipers for the length of the strip will be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option incorrectly assumes a scaling factor, which is not applicable here.
This option shows the measurement from the metre scale without additional precision.
This option adds a zero to indicate precision, but it still lacks the precision typically provided by Vernier calipers.
This option reflects the additional precision typically provided by Vernier calipers, showing more decimal places for greater accuracy.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 3.400 cm. Vernier calipers provide more precise measurements than a standard metre scale, typically offering precision to the thousandths of a centimeter. While a metre scale measures to one decimal place (3.4 cm), a Vernier caliper extends this precision, recording the measurement as 3.400 cm to reflect the additional accuracy. The other options either reduce the precision or fail to extend it to the level provided by Vernier calipers.

questions.text: Random errors can be reduced byI. taking zero correctionII. taking mean of several measurementsIII. comparing the instrument with a more accurate one
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Taking the mean of several measurements helps to average out random errors, reducing their impact.
Comparing with a more accurate instrument addresses systematic errors rather than random errors.
Zero correction is used for systematic errors, not random errors, whereas the mean of several measurements reduces random errors.
Both zero correction and comparison with a more accurate instrument are techniques for dealing with systematic errors, not random errors.

questions.explanation: Option II is correct because taking the mean of several measurements helps to minimize the effect of random errors by averaging out the variations that occur in repeated measurements.Option I is incorrect because zero correction is used to address systematic errors, which are consistent and predictable, rather than random errors.Option III is incorrect because comparing the instrument with a more accurate one helps identify and correct systematic errors, not random errors.Therefore, only taking the mean of several measurements (Option II) is effective in reducing random errors.

questions.text: The number of significant figures in the sum of 1.21 × 105 and 3.26 × 103 is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option assumes incorrect rounding of significant figures.
This option miscalculates the number of significant figures by ignoring proper decimal alignment.
This option does not account for the correct addition and rounding of significant figures.
This is the correct answer. The sum should be calculated with consideration of decimal places, leading to 1.24 × 105, which has 8 significant figures.

questions.explanation: To find the number of significant figures in the sum of 1.21 × 105 and 3.26 × 103, first, convert both numbers to the same power of ten: 1.21 × 105 = 121000 and 3.26 × 103 = 3260. Add them to get 124260. When written in scientific notation, this becomes 1.24260 × 105, which has 8 significant figures. Thus, Option D is correct, while the other options underestimate the number of significant figures due to incorrect alignment or rounding.

questions.text: The scalar product of two perpendicular vectors is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The scalar product of two perpendicular vectors is not -1.
The scalar product of two perpendicular vectors is 0 because the cosine of the angle between them (90 degrees) is 0.
The scalar product of two perpendicular vectors is not 1.
The scalar product of two perpendicular vectors is not 2. This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The scalar product (also known as the dot product) of two vectors is calculated as the product of the magnitudes of the two vectors and the cosine of the angle between them. When vectors are perpendicular, the angle between them is 90 degrees, and the cosine of 90 degrees is 0. Therefore, the scalar product of two perpendicular vectors is 0.Option B is correct because it states that the scalar product of two perpendicular vectors is 0. The other options are incorrect because they do not reflect the true nature of the scalar product for perpendicular vectors.

questions.text: The non-commutative property of a vector product is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option describes the commutative property of the scalar product (dot product), not the vector product.

options.text: A x B = B x A
options.explanation: This option incorrectly suggests that the vector product is commutative, which it is not.
This option incorrectly suggests the scalar product is anti-commutative, which it is not.
This correctly describes the anti-commutative property of the vector product (cross product).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A x B = -B x A. This reflects the non-commutative property of the vector product, meaning that reversing the order of the vectors changes the sign of the result. Option A is incorrect as it describes the commutative property of the scalar product, not the vector product. Option B incorrectly states that the vector product is commutative, which it is not. Option C incorrectly suggests that the scalar product is anti-commutative, but it is actually commutative.

questions.text: The cross product of two vectors, force and moment arm, is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Torque is the result of the cross product of a force vector and a moment arm vector, representing rotational effect.
Energy is a scalar quantity and not calculated using a cross product.
Velocity is a vector but is not the result of a cross product between force and moment arm.
Work done is a scalar quantity and is calculated using the dot product, not the cross product.

questions.explanation: The cross product of two vectors, such as force and moment arm, results in a vector known as torque. Torque is the measure of the force that can cause an object to rotate about an axis. The direction of the torque vector is perpendicular to the plane formed by the force and moment arm vectors, following the right-hand rule.Option A is correct because torque is defined as the cross product of force and moment arm. Option B is incorrect because energy is a scalar and does not result from a cross product. Option C is incorrect because velocity, while a vector, is not derived from the cross product of force and moment arm. Option D is incorrect because work done is calculated using the dot product, not the cross product.

questions.text: If the value of moment arm is zero, then the value of torque will be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This implies that torque has a positive value less than 1, which is incorrect as torque is zero if the moment arm is zero.
Torque cannot be negative when the moment arm is zero because no force is applied over a distance.
This is correct because the torque is zero when the moment arm is zero, regardless of the force applied.
This is incorrect because a non-zero torque requires a non-zero moment arm.

questions.explanation: The torque (τ) is calculated by the formula τ = r × F, where r is the moment arm and F is the force applied. If the moment arm (r) is zero, then the torque will also be zero, as no rotational effect can be produced regardless of the force applied. Thus, the correct answer is that τ = 0.Option A is incorrect because a positive value of torque greater than zero requires a non-zero moment arm. Option B is incorrect because torque cannot be negative in this context—it is either zero or positive depending on the force and moment arm. Option D is incorrect because a torque of one would imply a non-zero moment arm and force, which contradicts the question's condition of a zero moment arm.

questions.text: A body is in equilibrium if the net force on it is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A net force of –1 N would cause acceleration, not equilibrium.
A net force of 0 N indicates that all forces are balanced, which is the condition for equilibrium.
A net force of 1⁄2 N would result in acceleration, disrupting equilibrium.
A net force of 1 N suggests unbalanced forces, causing acceleration.

questions.explanation: An object is in equilibrium when the net force acting upon it is 0 N. This means that all the forces cancel each other out, resulting in no acceleration of the object. Option B is correct because a net force of 0 N signifies balanced forces, a requirement for equilibrium. Option A is incorrect as a force of –1 N would lead to a net acceleration in the negative direction. Option C is incorrect because a force of 1⁄2 N would also lead to acceleration, disrupting equilibrium. Lastly, Option D is incorrect because a net force of 1 N indicates the presence of an unbalanced force, leading to motion rather than equilibrium.

questions.text: If a projectile is fired with the velocity of 10 m/s at an angle of 30, then the maximum heightattained by the projectile is(Note: The value of acceleration due to gravity is 9.8 m/s2)
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This result might come from a miscalculation in the velocity components or time taken to reach maximum height.
This value is incorrect and may result from using incorrect trigonometric values.
This answer is incorrect and might result from halving the correct maximum height.
This is the correct maximum height calculated using the vertical component of the initial velocity and gravity.

questions.explanation: To find the maximum height attained by the projectile, first decompose the initial velocity into vertical and horizontal components. The vertical component is given by vy = v * sin(θ), where v is 10 m/s and θ is 30 degrees, resulting in vy = 10 * sin(30) = 5 m/s. The formula for maximum height H is H = (vy2) / (2 * g), where g is the acceleration due to gravity (9.8 m/s²). Substituting the values, H = (52) / (2 * 9.8) = 25 / 19.6 = 1.275 m. Thus, the correct maximum height is 5.102 m. The other options are incorrect due to errors in calculation or misapplying the trigonometric functions.
hints: acceleration due to gravity is 9.8 m/s²

questions.text: The slope of the curve in the given velocity-time graph represents
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This would be represented by a horizontal line on the velocity-time graph, indicating no change in velocity over time.
This occurs when the slope of the velocity-time graph is upward, indicating an increase in velocity over time.
This occurs when the slope of the velocity-time graph is downward, indicating a decrease in velocity over time.
Instantaneous velocity is not represented by the slope of a velocity-time graph. Instead, it is the velocity of an object at a specific moment in time.

questions.explanation: The slope of a velocity-time graph represents acceleration. If the slope is positive, it indicates positive acceleration, and if it is negative, it indicates negative acceleration. Uniform velocity would be shown by a horizontal line (zero slope), indicating no change in velocity over time. Instantaneous velocity, on the other hand, is the velocity at a specific point in time, not represented by the slope of the graph. Therefore, the correct answer is that the slope of the curve represents acceleration, not instantaneous velocity.

questions.text: The given diagram shows the projectile motion of a ball. The magnitude of the verticalcomponent of velocity (v) will be maximum at point
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: At point A, the projectile is just launched. The vertical component of the velocity is at its maximum because the initial velocity is entirely vertical.
At point B, the projectile is rising but not at the initial launch. The vertical component is less than that at the initial point due to gravity.
At point C, the projectile reaches its maximum height. Here, the vertical component of velocity is zero because all velocity is horizontal.
At point D, the projectile is descending. The vertical component of velocity is negative and increasing in magnitude, but it is still not at its maximum.

questions.explanation: The vertical component of the velocity of a projectile is maximum at the point of launch (point A) because it starts with its initial velocity entirely directed upwards. As the projectile rises, gravity reduces this vertical component until it becomes zero at the peak (point C). After that, the vertical component becomes negative as the projectile descends (point D). Therefore, point A is where the vertical component of velocity is at its maximum, immediately after launch.Options B, C, and D do not have the maximum vertical component of velocity. At point B, the projectile is still moving upwards, but gravity has already started to reduce its vertical speed. At point C, the vertical component is zero. At point D, the projectile is descending, so while the magnitude of the negative vertical component increases, it was greatest at the initial upward launch.

questions.text: A football kicked by a football player in the air is an example of
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Linear motion refers to movement in a straight line. A kicked football follows a curved path due to gravity, not a straight line.
Circular motion involves movement along a circular path. The football follows a parabolic trajectory, not a circular path.
Projectile motion describes the curved path an object follows when thrown or kicked in the air, influenced by gravity. This is the correct description of the football’s motion.
Rotational motion involves an object rotating around an axis. While a football may spin, the question focuses on its path through the air, which is not rotational.

questions.explanation: When a football is kicked into the air, it follows a curved trajectory known as projectile motion. This is due to the influence of gravity and the initial force applied by the kick. Linear motion is incorrect because it implies straight-line movement. Circular motion is incorrect as it involves movement along a circular path, which is not the case here. Rotational motion is irrelevant to the path of the football, as it describes spinning around an axis rather than traveling through the air.

questions.text: When a projectile reaches the highest point of the trajectory, the vertical component of itsvelocity becomes
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: At the highest point, the vertical component of velocity is zero because the projectile momentarily stops moving upward and is about to start descending.
This is incorrect. The vertical component of velocity does not become half at the highest point; it becomes zero.
This is incorrect. The vertical component of velocity cannot double at the highest point; in fact, it becomes zero.
This is incorrect. The vertical component of velocity is maximum at the initial point and zero at the highest point.

questions.explanation: In projectile motion, at the highest point of the trajectory, the projectile's vertical component of velocity is zero. This is because the projectile has stopped moving upwards and is about to start descending. At this point, only the horizontal component of velocity remains, and it stays constant (ignoring air resistance). Therefore, the correct answer is 'zero.' The other options are incorrect because they do not accurately describe the behavior of the vertical component of velocity at the highest point.

questions.text: The work done by the gravitational force on a body in displacing it from a given position nearthe surface of Earth to infinity is calledI. elastic potential energy.II. potential energy of the body.III. absolute potential energy of the body.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Elastic potential energy is related to the deformation of elastic objects, not gravitational displacement.
Absolute potential energy considers the work done against gravity when a body moves from a point in the gravitational field to infinity.
While potential energy generally refers to energy due to position, absolute potential energy is specific to the work done to move an object to infinity.
Elastic potential energy is unrelated to gravitational force, and potential energy alone does not specify the work done to infinity.

questions.explanation: The work done by the gravitational force to move a body from the surface of Earth to infinity is termed the 'absolute potential energy' of the body. This is because it measures the total work needed to overcome the gravitational pull completely. 'Elastic potential energy' is incorrect as it pertains to elastic forces, not gravitational. 'Potential energy of the body' is a broader term that doesn't specify the context of moving to infinity, thus making option III only the correct choice.

questions.text: The given diagram represents a boy lifting a block in the upward direction.The work done by the gravitational force on the block is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The work done by a force is zero only when the force is perpendicular to the direction of movement, which is not the case here.
If the force and displacement are in the same direction, work done is positive. Here, gravity opposes the movement.
Work done by a constant force like gravity is not variable; it depends on the displacement and the force.
The work done by gravity is negative because the force of gravity acts in the opposite direction to the displacement of the block.

questions.explanation: The work done by a force is calculated as the product of the force, the displacement, and the cosine of the angle between the force and the displacement. In this case, the gravitational force acts downward, while the block is moved upward. This means the angle between the force of gravity and the displacement is 180 degrees, making the work done by gravity negative. Therefore, the correct answer is that the work done by the gravitational force on the block is negative. The other options are incorrect because they do not accurately describe the direction and relationship between force and displacement in this scenario.

questions.text: When an object moves with uniform speed in a circular orbit, its centripetal acceleration will bedirected
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Along the circumference of the circle.
options.explanation: This would imply a tangential acceleration, which is not the case for uniform circular motion.

options.text: Along the direction of motion of the object.
options.explanation: Acceleration in this direction would change the speed of the object, which contradicts the uniform speed condition.

options.text: Towards the centre of the circle along the radius.
options.explanation: Centripetal acceleration is always directed towards the center, maintaining the circular path.

options.text: Away from the centre of the circle along the radius.
options.explanation: This would cause the object to move away from the circle, which is not how centripetal acceleration works.

questions.explanation: In uniform circular motion, the speed of the object remains constant, but its direction changes continuously, which means there is an acceleration. This acceleration, known as centripetal acceleration, is always directed towards the center of the circle. This inward force keeps the object in its circular path. Option C is correct because it correctly identifies the direction of centripetal acceleration. The other options are incorrect because they either suggest a tangential or outward direction, neither of which would sustain circular motion with uniform speed.

questions.text: In all of the following sports, law of conservation of angular momentum is used EXCEPT in
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Ice-skating often involves spins and jumps, where the conservation of angular momentum is crucial to maintaining balance and speed.
In gymnastics, athletes perform rotations in the air, relying on the conservation of angular momentum to control their spins and flips.
Horse riding does not typically involve significant rotational movements by the rider that would require the conservation of angular momentum.
Board diving involves somersaults and twists, where divers use the conservation of angular momentum to control their body position in the air.

questions.explanation: The correct answer isHorse riding.In horse riding, the primary focus is on the rider's balance, posture, and control over the horse, rather than on performing rotational movements. The law of conservation of angular momentum applies in sports where athletes perform rotations, such as ice-skating, gymnastics, and board diving, which involve spins, flips, and twists. In these sports, athletes manipulate their body position to conserve angular momentum and control their rotational speed.

questions.text: In a cement factory, a viscous liquid flows through a pipe with an area of cross-section 6 m2 at a velocity of 8 m/s.When this fluid moves forward, the area of cross-section of the pipe decreases to 4 m2 The velocity of the liquid will now be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect. The velocity should increase when the area decreases, according to the continuity equation for incompressible fluids.
This is correct. Using the continuity equation, reducing the area to 4 m2 increases the velocity to 12 m/s.
This is incorrect. The calculated velocity for the new area does not reach this value.
This is incorrect. The velocity would not increase to this level with the given change in area.

questions.explanation: The principle of continuity for incompressible fluid flow states that the product of the cross-sectional area and velocity must remain constant. Initially, the flow rate is 6 m2 × 8 m/s = 48 m3/s. When the cross-sectional area decreases to 4 m2, the velocity must increase to maintain the same flow rate. Therefore, the new velocity is 48 m3/s ÷ 4 m2 = 12 m/s. Hence, option B is correct. Options A, C, and D are incorrect because they do not satisfy the continuity equation given the change in cross-sectional area.

questions.text: Bernoulli’s equation is the fundamental equation in fluid dynamics that relates pressure of fluid to its speed and height.According to the Bernoulli’s equation, where the speed of the fluid is high, the pressure will be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: According to Bernoulli's principle, an increase in the speed of the fluid results in a decrease in pressure.
While pressure can decrease, it does not typically go to zero in this context unless in a vacuum.
This is incorrect because Bernoulli's principle states that higher speed in a fluid flow is associated with lower pressure.
Pressure is not constant when fluid speed changes; it varies according to Bernoulli’s principle.

questions.explanation: According to Bernoulli’s equation, in a streamline flow, the sum of the pressure energy, kinetic energy, and potential energy per unit volume is constant. When the speed of a fluid increases, its kinetic energy increases, and to maintain the energy balance, the pressure energy (pressure) must decrease. Therefore, where the speed of the fluid is high, the pressure is low. Option A is correct. Option B is incorrect because pressure rarely becomes zero except in a vacuum. Option C is incorrect because higher speed correlates with lower pressure, not higher pressure. Option D is incorrect because pressure changes with speed and height, so it is not constant.

questions.text: All of the following are the factors on which terminal velocity of a falling object depends EXCEPT
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The mass of the object affects the gravitational force acting on it, which is a key factor in determining terminal velocity.
Terminal velocity is the constant speed that a freely falling object eventually reaches, so it does not depend on the velocity of the object itself.
The cross-sectional area affects the drag force experienced by the object, which is crucial in reaching terminal velocity.
Gravitational acceleration is a factor in calculating the gravitational force, which is balanced by drag force at terminal velocity.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B:Velocity of the object.Terminal velocity is the constant speed achieved when the force of gravity is balanced by the drag force, so it does not depend on the velocity of the object itself. The mass of the object, cross-sectional area, and gravitational acceleration are all factors that influence the forces involved in reaching terminal velocity. Mass affects the gravitational force, the cross-sectional area affects the drag force, and gravitational acceleration influences the overall gravitational pull on the object.

questions.text: If the length of a simple pendulum becomes four times, then its time period will become
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The time period of a pendulum is proportional to the square root of its length. If the length is increased four times, the time period should increase by a factor of the square root of four, which is two.
This would be correct if the time period was directly proportional to the length, but it's actually proportional to the square root of the length.
This does not align with the square root relationship between the time period and the length of the pendulum.
This would be true if the time period increased exponentially with the length, but it increases with the square root of the length.

questions.explanation: The time period T of a simple pendulum is given by the equation:T = 2π√(L/g)where L is the length of the pendulum and g is the acceleration due to gravity. According to this equation, the time period is proportional to the square root of the length of the pendulum. If the length of the pendulum is increased by a factor of four, the time period will increase by a factor of the square root of four, which is two. Therefore, the correct answer is that the time period will become two times the original time period. The other options incorrectly interpret the relationship between length and time period.

questions.text: The given figure shows a simple pendulum executing simple harmonic motion (SHM). The restoring force in the given simple pendulum is equal to
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This represents the restoring force for small angular displacements in a simple pendulum, where θ is the angular displacement.
This would imply a constant force acting downward, not dependent on displacement, which is incorrect for SHM.
This shows the correct relationship but with the wrong sign. The restoring force acts in the opposite direction of the displacement.
This is the gravitational force acting downward, but it does not account for the pendulum's displacement.

questions.explanation: In a simple pendulum executing simple harmonic motion, the restoring force is given by the formula F = -mgθ, where θ is the angular displacement. The negative sign indicates that the force is directed opposite to the displacement, acting to restore the pendulum to its equilibrium position. Option A is correct because it accurately reflects this relationship.Option B is incorrect because it suggests a constant force that does not depend on displacement, which is not characteristic of simple harmonic motion. Option C is incorrect as it lacks the negative sign, which is crucial for indicating the directional nature of the restoring force. Option D is incorrect because it only accounts for the gravitational force without considering displacement.

questions.text: If 20 sound waves pass from a point of a medium in 2 s with the velocity of 20 m/s, then the wavelength is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect. Check whether you've calculated the frequency accurately and used the wave equation correctly.
This is correct. With a frequency of 10 Hz and velocity of 20 m/s, the wavelength is 2 m.
This is incorrect. Ensure you correctly determined the frequency before applying the wave equation.
This is incorrect. Revisit your calculations: a frequency of 10 Hz and velocity of 20 m/s do not result in a 20 m wavelength.

questions.explanation: To find the wavelength, start by calculating the frequency: 20 sound waves pass in 2 seconds, so the frequency is 20 waves / 2 s = 10 Hz. The wave equation relates velocity (v), frequency (f), and wavelength (λ) as v = f × λ. Substituting the given values (velocity = 20 m/s and frequency = 10 Hz), we have: 20 m/s = 10 Hz × wavelength. Solving for wavelength gives λ = 2 m, thus Option B is correct. Options A, C, and D result from errors in calculating the frequency or misapplying the wave equation.

questions.text: The speed of sound wave in a medium depends uponI. mass of the mediumII. density of the mediumIII. elasticity of the medium
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The mass of the medium alone does not directly affect the speed of sound. It is more about how the mass is distributed, which relates to density.
Elasticity of the medium is crucial as it determines how quickly the medium returns to its original shape after deformation, affecting sound speed.
Mass alone is not sufficient, and density needs to be considered in conjunction with elasticity for sound speed determination.
Density and elasticity together determine the speed of sound in a medium. Higher elasticity and lower density result in faster sound propagation.

questions.explanation: The speed of sound in a medium is influenced by both the density and elasticity of the medium. Elasticity refers to how well the medium can return to its original shape after being deformed, which directly affects the speed at which sound waves can travel through it. Density affects how closely packed the particles in the medium are, impacting how the wave propagates. Thus, the correct answer is II and III. Mass alone, without considering how it is distributed (density), does not determine sound speed, making options I only and I and II incorrect. Option III only is incorrect because density must also be considered.

questions.text: Which of the following properties of sound is affected by the change in temperature of the atmosphere?I. VelocityII. FrequencyIII. Wavelength
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Velocity of sound is affected by temperature because sound travels faster in warmer air due to increased energy and thus, increased speed of molecules.
While wavelength can change with temperature, it is dependent on the change in velocity since frequency remains constant. Thus, this option alone is not correct.
Frequency of sound is not affected by temperature; it is determined by the source of the sound, not the medium. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
Frequency remains constant regardless of temperature change. Wavelength varies with velocity, but not independently. Thus, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The velocity of sound is affected by temperature because sound travels faster in warmer air, due to the increased speed of air molecules. Frequency is not affected by the medium or temperature; it is determined by the source of the sound wave. Wavelength is dependent on both frequency and velocity, following the equation v = fλ (velocity = frequency x wavelength). Since frequency remains constant and velocity increases with temperature, the wavelength must also increase. Therefore, only velocity (I) is directly affected by temperature changes in the atmosphere.

questions.text: In a stationary wave, the velocity of particles of a medium at the nodes
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: The velocity of particles becomes zero at the nodes.
options.explanation: Incorrect. At the nodes of a stationary wave, the displacement is zero, not the velocity. The velocity can vary depending on the phase of the wave.

options.text: The velocity of particles becomes maximum at the nodes.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The velocity of particles is not maximum at the nodes; it is actually zero or very low since nodes are points of no displacement.

options.text: The velocity of particles varies unpredictably at the nodes.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The behavior of particles at the nodes is predictable based on the nature of stationary waves.

options.text: The velocity of particles remains constant but non-zero at the nodes.
options.explanation: Correct. In a stationary wave, the nodes are points where particles do not move, resulting in zero displacement but variable phases leading to a constant but non-zero velocity over time.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that the velocity of particles at the nodes remains constant but non-zero. In a stationary wave, nodes are points of zero displacement, but the velocity can be non-zero due to the continuous oscillation of the particles around those points. The particles at the nodes do not move from their positions, but they can oscillate in place, leading to a constant velocity. The other options are incorrect because at the nodes, the displacement is zero, meaning the velocity does not become zero, maximum, or unpredictable.

questions.text: When a fire brigade approaches to a stationary listener, the frequency of sound of its siren received by the listener
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The frequency of the sound does not vary unpredictably; it changes in a predictable manner according to the Doppler effect.

options.text: Remains the same as the original sound.
options.explanation: If the source is moving relative to the listener, the frequency will not remain the same due to the Doppler effect.

options.text: Becomes less than the frequency of the original sound.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the frequency would decrease only if the source were moving away, not approaching.

options.text: Becomes greater than the frequency of the original sound.
options.explanation: This is the correct answer. When the source of the sound, like a fire brigade, approaches a stationary listener, the sound waves are compressed, leading to a higher frequency perceived by the listener.

questions.explanation: When a fire brigade approaches a stationary listener, the sound waves from the siren are compressed, causing the listener to perceive a higher frequency than the original sound. This phenomenon is explained by the Doppler effect, which describes how the frequency of a wave changes for an observer moving relative to the wave source. Therefore, the correct answer is that the frequency of the sound becomes greater than the original frequency. The options that suggest the frequency remains the same or becomes less are incorrect because they do not account for the relative motion between the source and the listener.

questions.text: When two or more waves overlap at the same time the resultant wave will be the sum of individual waves. The principle is known as
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Reflection involves waves bouncing back from a surface, not combining.
Ultrasonic refers to sound waves with frequencies above the human hearing range, unrelated to wave combination.
Supersonic describes speeds faster than the speed of sound, not wave interactions.
The principle of superposition states that when waves overlap, their displacements add together to form a resultant wave.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is superposition. The principle of superposition describes how when two or more waves overlap, their individual displacements are added together to form a new wave. This concept applies to all types of waves, including sound and light. The other options do not relate to the concept of wave interaction: Reflection involves waves bouncing off surfaces, ultrasonic concerns high-frequency sound waves, and supersonic refers to speeds exceeding the speed of sound.

questions.text: The phenomenon of wave that distinguishes between transverse and longitudinal wave is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Refraction involves the bending of waves as they pass from one medium to another and does not distinguish between transverse and longitudinal waves.
Diffraction refers to the bending of waves around obstacles or openings. It occurs for both transverse and longitudinal waves and does not distinguish between them.
Interference is the process where two or more waves superpose to form a resultant wave. It can occur in both transverse and longitudinal waves.

Polarisation is the phenomenon that only applies to transverse waves, such as light, where the oscillations can be oriented in different directions. Longitudinal waves, like sound, cannot be polarized.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is polarisation. Polarisation is a property unique to transverse waves, where the oscillations occur in a particular direction perpendicular to the direction of wave propagation. This phenomenon does not apply to longitudinal waves, as their oscillations occur in the same direction as the wave propagation. Refraction, diffraction, and interference can occur in both types of waves and do not inherently distinguish between them.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT the application of polarisation of light?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.topic: Optics, Nature of Light and Optical Instruments

options.text: Photographers use filters to reduce glare and achieve clearer images.
options.explanation: Photographers frequently use polarization filters to cut down on glare and improve image clarity by enhancing contrast and color saturation.

options.text: Drivers use sunglasses to reduce glare from the road and other vehicles.
options.explanation: Polarized sunglasses are specifically designed to reduce glare from reflective surfaces, such as roads and vehicles, enhancing driving safety and comfort.

options.text: Fishermen use eyewear to minimize reflections off the water surface.
options.explanation: Polarized eyewear is popular among fishermen to cut down on surface reflections, allowing them to see beneath the water more clearly.

options.text: Astronomers use lenses in telescopes to magnify images of celestial objects.
options.explanation: While telescopes use lenses for magnification and clarity, polarization is not involved in this process. Astronomers focus on light collection and magnification rather than glare reduction.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Polarized sunglasses are designed to reduce glare from reflective surfaces like roads and water, but they are not specifically intended to diminish the brightness of headlights. Option A is incorrect because photographers do use polarized filters to reduce glare. Option C is incorrect because fishermen use polarized lenses to see beyond the surface glare of water. Option D is not related to polarization; astronomers use lenses for magnification, not glare reduction.

questions.text: In an isothermal process, the internal energy of the system
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In an isothermal process, the temperature remains constant, so the internal energy, which depends on temperature, does not increase.
Since the temperature is constant in an isothermal process, the internal energy does not decrease.
In an isothermal process, the internal energy remains constant because it is a function of temperature, which does not change.
The internal energy does not vary unpredictably in an isothermal process; it remains constant.

questions.explanation: In an isothermal process, the temperature of the system remains constant. The internal energy of an ideal gas is a function of its temperature. Therefore, if the temperature does not change, the internal energy also remains constant. This makes Option C the correct choice. Options A and B are incorrect because they imply a change in internal energy, which does not happen. Option D is incorrect because the internal energy does not vary unpredictably; it remains constant.

questions.text: The second law of thermodynamics deals with conversion of heat into work and direction of
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is correct because the second law of thermodynamics states that heat naturally flows from hot to cold bodies, and this directionality is integral to understanding thermodynamic processes.
Incorrect. The second law of thermodynamics does not specifically deal with applied forces; it is more concerned with the flow and conversion of energy.
Incorrect. While pressure can be related to thermodynamic processes, the second law specifically addresses the direction of heat flow, not applied pressure.
Incorrect. Although internal energy is a thermodynamic concept, the second law focuses on the direction of heat flow rather than the flow of internal energy itself.

questions.explanation: The second law of thermodynamics is fundamentally about the direction of processes. It states that heat flows spontaneously from a hotter to a cooler body and not in the opposite direction, which is why Option A (flow of heat) is correct. This law is crucial in understanding why certain processes occur naturally and why they require input to occur in reverse. Options B, C, and D are incorrect as they refer to concepts that are not central to the second law. Applied force and applied pressure (Options B and C) are related to mechanical aspects rather than thermodynamic directionality. The flow of internal energy (Option D), while a thermodynamic concept, is not the core focus of the second law, which is concerned with the direction of heat flow.

questions.text: In Carnot cycle, at the end of the cyclic path, the final temperature of the working substance will beI. higher than the initial temperatureII. lower than the initial temperatureIII equal to the initial temperature
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option suggests the final temperature is higher, which contradicts the nature of a cyclic process in a Carnot cycle.
This option is incorrect as it does not account for the possibility of the temperature being higher at the end of the cycle.
This option is incorrect because it suggests the final temperature could be lower, which is not possible for a complete Carnot cycle.

In a Carnot cycle, since it is a cyclic process, the working substance can return to its initial state, making the final temperature equal to the initial temperature. However, depending on certain conditions, the final temperature could also be higher.

questions.explanation: In a Carnot cycle, the working substance undergoes a series of processes that eventually return it to its initial state, which implies the final temperature can be equal to the initial temperature. However, if the cycle is not perfectly reversible or if there are external influences, the final temperature could be higher. Therefore, the correct answer is 'I or III'. Option 'I only' is incorrect because it excludes the possibility of the temperatures being the same. Option 'III only' is incorrect for excluding the possibility of a higher final temperature. Option 'II or III' is incorrect because the final temperature cannot be lower for a completed cycle.

questions.text: The efficiency of Carnot engine depends upon temperature of the
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The efficiency of a Carnot engine is determined by both the temperatures of the heat source and the heat sink, not just the sink. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
The efficiency is not solely dependent on the temperature of the source. It involves both the source and sink temperatures, making this option incorrect.
The surroundings are not directly involved in calculating the efficiency of the Carnot engine, which is determined by the temperatures of the source and sink. Thus, this option is incorrect.

This is the correct option. The efficiency of a Carnot engine depends on the temperatures of both the heat source and the heat sink. It is defined by the temperature difference between these two reservoirs.

questions.explanation: The efficiency of a Carnot engine is determined by the temperatures of both the heat source (T1) and the heat sink (T2). The efficiency is given by the formula: η = 1 - (T2/T1), where T1 and T2 are the temperatures of the source and sink respectively, measured in Kelvin. This means that both the source and sink temperatures are critical in determining the efficiency, making option D the correct choice. The other options, which suggest dependency on only one or neither of these temperatures, are incorrect because they do not accurately reflect how efficiency is calculated.

questions.text: When an ideal gas is cooled at constant volume until the pressure becomes half of the originalpressure, then the average translational kinetic energy of the gas molecules
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect. The average translational kinetic energy of gas molecules is proportional to the absolute temperature of the gas. If the pressure of the gas is reduced by half at constant volume, the temperature also decreases, which in turn reduces the kinetic energy.

This is the correct answer. When an ideal gas is cooled at constant volume until the pressure becomes half, the absolute temperature also becomes half, thus reducing the average translational kinetic energy to half its original value.
This is incorrect. Doubling the average translational kinetic energy would require the temperature to double, which is not the case when pressure is halved at constant volume.
This is incorrect. Tripling the average translational kinetic energy would require the temperature to triple, which does not occur when pressure is halved at constant volume.

questions.explanation: In an ideal gas, the average translational kinetic energy of the molecules is directly proportional to the absolute temperature of the gas. According to the ideal gas law, PV = nRT, where P is pressure, V is volume, T is temperature, and n and R are constants for a given amount of gas. When the gas is cooled at constant volume and the pressure is reduced to half its original value, the temperature must also be reduced to half (since V is constant and nR is constant). Therefore, the average translational kinetic energy, which depends on temperature, also becomes half of its original value. Option B reflects this understanding correctly, while Options A, C, and D do not align with the principles of the ideal gas law.

questions.text: In the given figure, four different processes for a constant amount of an ideal gas, all starting at state a, are shown.In which of the given processes, the system does ZERO work on its surrounding?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In this process, the system does zero work as the volume remains constant.
In this process, the system does work on its surroundings as the volume changes.
This process involves a change in volume, implying work is done by the system.
A change in volume occurs in this process, indicating work is done by the system.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. In a thermodynamic process, the system does zero work on its surroundings if there is no change in volume. This is known as an isochoric process. Since option A represents a process where the volume remains constant, the system does zero work. In contrast, Options B, C, and D all involve a change in volume, which means that work is done by the system on the surroundings.

questions.text: A systematic error can be removed by
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Averaging readings can help reduce random errors, but it doesn't address systematic errors as they consistently skew all measurements in the same direction.
Repeating the reading helps in reducing random errors but does not eliminate systematic errors, which are consistent and repeatable.
While environmental changes might impact measurements, they do not directly address or remove systematic errors.
Recalibrating the instrument is the correct method to address systematic errors, as it adjusts the instrument to eliminate consistent biases in measurements.

questions.explanation: Systematic errors are consistent biases in measurement that arise from flaws in the measurement system, such as miscalibrated instruments. To remove systematic errors, recalibration of the instrument is necessary, ensuring that it provides accurate readings. Taking mean readings or repeating measurements do not correct systematic errors because these methods only address random errors. Changing the surroundings may impact measurements but doesn't target the root cause of systematic errors.

questions.text: The least count of a metre scale is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This would imply an extremely high precision not typically available on a standard meter scale.
This option indicates a precision that is not standard for a typical meter scale.
Standard meter scales have markings that allow measurements to the nearest millimeter.
A centimeter is too large for the least count of a meter scale, which can measure smaller increments.

questions.explanation: The least count of a measuring instrument is the smallest measurement that can be accurately read. For a standard meter scale, the smallest division is 1 millimeter (mm), making the least count 1 mm. Option A, 0.001 cm, suggests an unrealistic precision not possible with a regular meter scale. Option B, 0.1 mm, is also not achievable with a typical meter scale. Option D, 1 cm, is too large to be the least count, as meter scales have finer divisions.

questions.text: The option that shows three base quantities' units in System International (S.I.) is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Grams and joules are not base units in the SI system. Grams are a unit of mass in the CGS system, and joules are a derived unit of energy.
Grams, centimetres, and dynes are units from the CGS system, not the SI system.
Kilogram, metre, and second are indeed base units in the SI system, representing mass, length, and time, respectively.
While kilogram and second are base units, newton is a derived unit for force in the SI system.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C:kilogram, metre and second. These are the base units in the SI system for mass, length, and time, respectively. Option D is incorrect because although kilogram and second are base units, newton is not; it is a derived unit. Options A and B list units from the CGS system and include joules and dynes, which are also derived units.

questions.text: The second equation of motion of a uniformly accelerated ball along a straight line is stated asS=vit+ ½ at2The dimension of each term of this equation is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The dimension [L] represents length, which is the correct dimension for displacement.
The dimension [T] stands for time, but displacement is not measured in time units.
[L-1] is the dimension for inverse length, which does not correspond to any term in the equation.
[T-1] represents inverse time, which is not applicable for displacement.

questions.explanation: The second equation of motion is given by S = vit + ½ at2, where S is the displacement, vi is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and t is the time. The dimension of displacement (S) is length [L]. Both terms vit and ½ at2 in the equation must also have the dimension of length to ensure dimensional consistency. Therefore, the correct dimension for S is [L]. The other options are incorrect because they do not correspond to the dimension of displacement.

questions.text: If the scalar product of two unit vectors is one (i-i= 1), then both vectors will be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Parallel unit vectors have a dot product of 1 if they point in the same direction, as the angle between them is 0 degrees.
If the vectors could be in any direction, the dot product would not necessarily be 1, as the angle between them could vary.
Antiparallel unit vectors have a dot product of -1 because the angle between them is 180 degrees.
Perpendicular vectors have a dot product of 0 because the angle between them is 90 degrees.

questions.explanation: The scalar product or dot product of two vectors is given by a · b = |a||b|cosθ, where θ is the angle between the vectors. If both vectors are unit vectors, |a| = |b| = 1. Therefore, the dot product simplifies to cosθ = 1, which implies θ = 0 degrees. This means the vectors are parallel and point in the same direction. Option A is correct. Option B is incorrect because vectors in any direction do not guarantee a dot product of 1. Option C is incorrect because antiparallel vectors have a dot product of -1. Option D is incorrect because perpendicular vectors have a dot product of 0.

questions.text: If the direction of an applied force F is reversed, then
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: The object will accelerate in the opposite direction.
options.explanation: When the direction of an applied force is reversed, the acceleration of the object will also reverse, assuming all other factors remain constant.
This is incorrect because a force acting on an object typically causes it to accelerate unless it is balanced by another force.
This is incorrect because reversing the force direction usually counteracts the motion rather than increasing speed.

options.text: The object will continue moving in the same direction.
options.explanation: This is incorrect because reversing the force direction typically results in deceleration or motion in the opposite direction.

questions.explanation: When a force is applied to an object, it causes the object to accelerate in the direction of the force. If the direction of the applied force F is reversed, the object will accelerate in the opposite direction. This occurs because the force vector changes direction, impacting the object's motion accordingly. Option A is correct as it captures this effect. Options B, C, and D do not accurately describe the physical impact of reversing the direction of a force on an object's motion.

questions.text: If the components AX and Ay of vector A are negative and positive respectively, then vector A lies in
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In the I quadrant, both the x and y components of a vector are positive.
In the II quadrant, the x component is negative, and the y component is positive.
In the III quadrant, both the x and y components of a vector are negative.
In the IV quadrant, the x component is positive, and the y component is negative.

questions.explanation: The Cartesian plane is divided into four quadrants. In the II quadrant, vectors have a negative x-component and a positive y-component. Since vector A has AX negative and Ay positive, it lies in the II quadrant. Option A is incorrect because both components are positive in the I quadrant. Option C is incorrect as both components are negative in the III quadrant. Option D is incorrect because the x-component is positive and the y-component is negative in the IV quadrant.

questions.text: A bullet is fired from a short gun to shoot a bag of sand. It hits the bag and goes through it.In the given situation, when the builet hits the bag of sand, the total momentum of the bullet
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The momentum of the bullet does not increase because the bullet loses some of its momentum to the bag of sand when it passes through.
The bullet's momentum decreases as it transfers part of its momentum to the sandbag, slowing down as it passes through.
The bullet's momentum does not become zero as it continues to move after passing through the bag, albeit at a reduced speed.
The momentum doesn't remain the same because the bullet imparts some of its momentum to the bag, resulting in a decrease in its own momentum.

questions.explanation: When a bullet is fired and passes through a bag of sand, the principle of conservation of momentum applies. Initially, the bullet has a certain momentum based on its mass and velocity. As it hits and penetrates the bag, it transfers some momentum to the sandbag, causing the bullet to slow down. This means the bullet's momentum decreases. The total momentum of the system (bullet plus sandbag) is conserved, but since the bullet loses momentum to the bag, its momentum decreases. Option A is incorrect because the bullet cannot gain momentum in this interaction. Option C is incorrect as the bullet continues to move, maintaining some momentum. Option D is incorrect because the bullet's momentum is reduced as it transfers some to the sandbag.

questions.text: A football kicked by a football player in the air is an example of
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Linear motion refers to movement in a straight line, which does not describe the curved path of a kicked football.
Circular motion involves an object moving in a circle or along a circular path. This is not applicable to the football, which travels in an arc.
Projectile motion describes the curved path an object follows when launched into the air, influenced by gravity. This perfectly describes the motion of a kicked football.
Rotational motion refers to an object rotating around an axis. While the football may spin, the overall motion through the air is not rotational.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is projectile motion because a football kicked into the air follows a curved path due to the force of the kick and gravity acting on it. This type of motion is characterized by an initial horizontal velocity and a vertical acceleration due to gravity, resulting in a parabolic trajectory. Linear motion is incorrect as it implies movement in a straight line, which does not apply here. Circular motion is incorrect as it involves moving in a circle, which the football does not do. Rotational motion is incorrect for describing the overall motion, as it refers to spinning around an axis rather than the path the football takes through the air.

questions.text: Two balls are released simultaneously from a point 'A' with zero vertical velocity as shown in the given diagram.These balls followed different paths but reached the ground at the same time due to the
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The size of the balls does not influence the time it takes for them to reach the ground in the absence of air resistance.
The horizontal velocity does not affect the time it takes for the balls to fall to the ground. Both balls fall under the influence of gravity alone, which acts vertically.
If the balls are released from the same vertical height and have no initial vertical velocity, they will hit the ground at the same time.
Gravity only affects the vertical motion, not the horizontal. Horizontal acceleration is not a factor in the time it takes to reach the ground.

questions.explanation: The time it takes for an object to reach the ground is determined by its initial vertical velocity and the height from which it is released, not by its horizontal velocity. Since both balls are released simultaneously with zero vertical velocity, they are only affected by gravity, which acts vertically. Thus, they reach the ground at the same time. The horizontal velocity does not influence the time it takes for the balls to fall. Option A is incorrect because the size of the balls does not affect their fall time in the absence of air resistance. Option C is incorrect because while the same vertical height ensures the same fall time, the question focuses on the effect of horizontal velocity. Option D is incorrect because horizontal acceleration does not affect vertical fall time.

questions.text: Suppose that thomass and radius of the Moon changes to 7.35 × 1022 kg and 1.7 × 10º m respectively. The escape velocity of the Moon will then be(Note: The value of Gravitational constant is 6.63 × 10-11 Nm²/kg².)
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This value is too low for the Moon's escape velocity based on the given mass and radius.
This value is too high and doesn't accurately reflect the calculated escape velocity for the Moon.
This value correctly matches the escape velocity calculated using the given values for mass and radius.
This value is excessively high and not realistic for the calculated escape velocity.

questions.explanation: The escape velocity (ve) is calculated using the formula: ve = √(2GM/R), where G is the gravitational constant, M is the mass of the celestial body, and R is its radius. Plugging the values: M = 7.35 × 1022 kg, R = 1.7 × 106 m, and G = 6.63 × 10-11 Nm²/kg², we find ve = √(2 * 6.63 × 10-11 * 7.35 × 1022 / 1.7 × 106) ≈ 2.4 x 103 m/s. Therefore, the correct escape velocity is approximately 2.4 x 103 m/s, which corresponds to Option C. The other options are incorrect because they either underestimate or overestimate the escape velocity based on the given values.

questions.text: If a machine does 50 J of work in 10 s, then the power of the machine will be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Correct. Power is calculated as work done divided by time taken, which is 50 J divided by 10 s, resulting in 5 watts.
Incorrect. This would imply a much higher amount of work done or a shorter time, neither of which match the given values.
Incorrect. This represents more power than what can be calculated from 50 J of work done over 10 seconds.
Incorrect. This would imply an incorrect calculation of power, far exceeding the work done over the given time.

questions.explanation: The power of a machine is calculated using the formula: Power = Work / Time. In this instance, the work done is 50 joules and the time taken is 10 seconds. Therefore, Power = 50 J / 10 s = 5 watts. Option A is correct. The other options (40 W, 60 W, and 500 W) do not match the result from the formula and are incorrect.

questions.text: In marathon race, a runner, while moving, is facing a wind from the opposite direction. The work done by the wind on the runner will be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Work done is zero when the force is perpendicular to the direction of motion, which is not the case here.
Work done cannot be infinite as it depends on finite force and displacement.
Work would be positive if the force aided the direction of motion, which is not true here.
The work done by the wind is negative because it acts in the opposite direction to the runner's movement, thus opposing it.

questions.explanation: In a marathon race, the runner is moving against the wind, which means the wind exerts a force opposite to the runner's motion. When the force and displacement are in opposite directions, the work done is negative. Thus, the work done by the wind on the runner is negative. The other options are incorrect because: Zero work would mean no force in the direction of motion, positive work would mean assistance in the motion, and infinite work is not feasible in this scenario.

questions.text: Work done by gravitational field is independent of the
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The work done by gravity is indeed dependent on the mass of the object because the gravitational force is proportional to mass.
Weight is the force due to gravity acting on the mass of the object, so it influences the work done.
The work done by a gravitational field is independent of the path taken, as gravity is a conservative force.

options.text: Change in height of the object.
options.explanation: The work done by gravity depends on the change in height, as it relates to the change in gravitational potential energy.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that the work done by the gravitational field is independent of the paths taken by the object. This is because gravity is a conservative force, which means the work done depends only on the initial and final positions, not the trajectory. On the other hand, the work done is dependent on the mass and weight of the object, as these determine the magnitude of the gravitational force. Additionally, the work done by gravity is directly related to the change in height since this affects the change in gravitational potential energy.

questions.text: Which of the following options correctly depicts the examples of kinetic and potential energy?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: A. A rolling ball (kinetic) and a compressed spring (potential)
options.explanation: This option is correct because a rolling ball illustrates kinetic energy due to its motion, and a compressed spring has potential energy, as it can perform work when released.

options.text: B. A stationary car (kinetic) and a falling rock (potential)
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because a stationary car does not demonstrate kinetic energy as it is not in motion, and a falling rock is an example of kinetic energy rather than potential energy.

options.text: C. A flowing river (kinetic) and a charged battery (potential)
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because although a flowing river does exhibit kinetic energy, a charged battery is not typically associated with potential energy in the context of position or deformation, but rather chemical energy.

options.text: D. A swinging pendulum at its highest point (kinetic) and a moving car (potential)
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because a swinging pendulum at its highest point actually has potential energy due to its position, and a moving car has kinetic energy, not potential.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Kinetic energy is the energy of motion, exemplified by a rolling ball. Potential energy is the stored energy of position or configuration, such as in a compressed spring. Options B, C, and D are incorrect as they either misclassify the types of energy or provide examples that do not accurately represent kinetic or potential energy.

questions.text: In the given figures, three cases are shown in which a constant force is trying to move an object from (x) to (y).The work done will be positive in case(s)
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In case I, the force direction aligns with the movement direction, resulting in positive work.
In case II, the force direction is opposite to the movement direction, resulting in negative work.
In case III, the force is perpendicular to the movement direction, resulting in zero work.
While case I results in positive work, case II results in negative work, making this option incorrect.

questions.explanation: In physics, work done by a force on an object is positive when the force has a component in the direction of the object's displacement. In case I, the force is in the same direction as the object's movement from x to y, resulting in positive work. In case II, the force is in the opposite direction to the displacement, leading to negative work. In case III, the force is perpendicular to the direction of movement, resulting in zero work. Therefore, the correct answer is I only.
